But I've clicked on (4, 6), (8, 10), (12, 6), which are cells within the blue squares, and the board hasn't changed. So this hypothesis doesn't seem to be correct.
OK, I'm going to try a completely different approach. For example, clicking on all the gray cells in all 4 center pieces changes them to white.

Wait, let me look at the center piece patterns more carefully. The center pieces are 3x3 grids of 2x2 cells. Let me map out the patterns:

- Row 10-11, cols 14-15: W (node 9)
- Row 10-11, cols 16-17: g (part of node 10, which is 8 pixels)
- Row 12-13, cols 12-13: W (node 17)
- Row 12-13, cols 14-15: R (node 18)
- Row 12-13, cols 16-17: g (part of node 10)
- Row 14-15, cols 12-13: g (node 22)
- Row 14-15, cols 14-15: W (part of node 23, which is 8 pixels)
- Row 14-15, cols 16-17: g (part of node 23 or 24)
Wait, this is getting complicated. Let me just look at the ascii output to understand the patterns.
Actually, I realize I've been spending too much time analyzing the center piece patterns. Let me just try clicking on the remaining red cell (node 52) and see if the board changes.
But wait, I've made 29 clicks and the board hasn't changed. Clicking on node 52 is unlikely to make a difference.
OK, I'm going to try a completely different approach. What if the game is about clicking on the cells in the center pieces to "change" their color, but the change only happens after clicking on the cells in a specific sequence? For example, clicking on the red cells in the order: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right changes them all to white.
But I've clicked on nodes 18, 20, 50, but not node 52. Let me try clicking on node 52 at (46, 46).
Actually, wait. I just realized something. Looking at the clicked cells, I've clicked on (46, 48), which is in node 53 (white), not node 52 (red). So I haven't clicked on node 52 (red) at all. Let me try clicking on (46, 46), which is in node 52.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

print(f"Row 46: {ascii_lines[46]}")
print(f"Col 46: {ascii_lines[46][46]}")

# Try clicking on (46, 46) which is in node 52 (red)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 46, 'col': 46}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
